attack_surface.data_description: Swap transaction parameters (input/output token, amounts, recipient)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters can be altered via a compromised interface before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with a dApp interface (e.g., BeraHub) to initiate a swap.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Reliance on the integrity of the dApp interface for critical transaction details.

assumptions: User interacts with a potentially compromised BeraHub or dApp interface.
Wallet does not provide clear, itemized breakdown of signed transaction details.
Attacker controls the interface presented to the user.
description: An attacker tampers with swap parameters (e.g., recipient address) via a compromised interface, redirecting funds.

security_gap.gap_description: Excessive transactions can congest contract processing or increase gas costs for others.

actor_risk.description: User sends a high volume of small transactions to the liquidity pool.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Potential for minor network or contract congestion and increased transaction costs.

assumptions: Transaction fees are low enough to make spamming economically feasible for an attacker.
The BEX contract processing is sensitive to transaction volume or frequency.

mitigation.strategy: Implement transaction rate limiting or dynamic fees.

description: An attacker spams small liquidity deposit and withdrawal operations on BEX, causing congestion.

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data is publicly visible on the blockchain explorer.

actor_risk.description: User participates in BGT earning activities.

behavioral_risk.rationale: This is an inherent characteristic of a public blockchain.

assumptions: User desires privacy regarding their specific BGT earning sources and amounts.
Blockchain explorers make this on-chain data easily accessible.
description: User's BGT claiming activity, including amount and source Reward Vault, is publicly visible on-chain.

attack_surface.data_description: BGT delegation transaction details (amount, target validator)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction data is publicly visible on the blockchain explorer.

behavioral_risk.rationale: This is an inherent characteristic of a public blockchain.

assumptions: User desires privacy for their BGT delegation strategy and amounts.
Blockchain explorers make this on-chain data easily accessible.
description: User's BGT delegation activity, including amount and target validator, is publicly visible on-chain.

attack_surface.data_description: Incentive claim proof (e.g., Merkle proof) and associated claim details

security_gap.gap_description: Claim relies on proofs generated by a potentially compromised off-chain backend service.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the incentive claiming mechanism.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Compromise of the off-chain proof generation system could lead to theft of user assets (Incentive Tokens).

assumptions: The backend service responsible for generating incentive claim proofs is compromised.
The on-chain verification relies on the integrity of the provided proof (e.g., Merkle proof).
An attacker can generate valid-looking proofs for claims they are not entitled to.

mitigation.strategy: Implement robust security controls for the backend proof generation service.

description: An attacker compromises the proof generation backend to forge claim proofs and steal other users' incentive tokens.

security_gap.gap_description: Rapid changes in delegation disrupt voting power calculations and potentially governance processes.

actor_risk.description: User controls a significant amount of BGT.

behavioral_risk.rationale: Potential to disrupt the stability and predictability of governance voting power.

assumptions: Attacker controls a substantial amount of BGT.
The system allows rapid delegation changes without significant penalty (e.g., cooldowns, high fees).
Governance processes are sensitive to sudden shifts in total delegated BGT or voting power distribution.

mitigation.strategy: Implement cooldown periods or increased costs for frequent BGT delegation changes.

description: An attacker with significant BGT disrupts governance by rapidly changing their delegation across validators.

attack_surface.data_description: Redemption transaction parameters (BGT amount, BERA recipient address)

security_gap.gap_description: Transaction parameters can be altered via a compromised interface before signing.

actor_risk.description: User interacts with the redemption interface (e.g., BeraHub).

behavioral_risk.rationale: Reliance on the integrity of the dApp interface for a high-value operation.

assumptions: User interacts with a potentially compromised BeraHub interface.
Wallet does not provide clear, itemized breakdown of signed transaction details.
Attacker controls the interface presented to the user.
description: An attacker tampers with BGT redemption parameters (e.g., BERA recipient address) via a compromised interface.
